items.title: G7 Leaders' Summit 2025: Day 1 wrap-up
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>It was a productive first day of the #G7 Leaders’ Summit, focused on shared goals of delivering concrete and lasting outcomes on global issues.  
#G7Kananaskis2025</article>

items.title: From Classroom to Global Boardroom: G7 Mock Summit
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>What happens when high school students take on the roles of world leaders? 
In this short video, students from Bow Valley, Alberta, talk about their experiences as G7 members during a mock summit, where they debated about global issues that matter to everyone. 
  
Hear directly from two students and a teacher as they reflect on the power of experiential learning. 
  
Learn more about Canada’s 2025 G7 Presidency at https://g7.canada.ca 
#G7Kananaskis2025 #G7Canada #YouthLeadership #MockSummit #StudentVoices</article>

items.title: Mother's Day 2025 - Bring Kids Back

items.content:
<article>Since 2022, thousands of children from Ukraine were illegally deported to Russia.  

Bring them back to their families and communities.  
#BringKidsBack 

https://international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/response_conflict-reponse_conflits/crisis-crises/ukraine-children-enfants.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.content: <article>His Excellency, Mr. Bob Rae, President of ECOSOC and Permanent Representative of Canada to the United Nations, delivers the closing remarks of the ECOSOC Financing for Development Panel Discussion, on Tuesday, 29 April 2025, at the United Nations Trusteeship Council.</article>

items.title: What is accreditation?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>Summits and other large-scale events rely on a process called accreditation to ensure their success and the safety of all participants.</article>

items.title: The road to Kananaskis series: What is the G7?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada

items.title: What are Tariffs?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>What are tariffs? 
Tariffs are taxes or duties that countries apply to imports. They don’t appear on sales receipts.  

How do tariffs affect me?  
Tariffs can impact the prices you pay at the store. When the cost of importing goods increases, businesses pass the bulk of this cost onto consumers.  

Why do Canada’s free trade agreements matter?  
Canada’s 15 free trade agreements provide Canadians with tariff-free access to nearly all products traded between Canada and its free-trade partners. This helps Canadian businesses access markets in over 50 countries representing 1.5 billion consumers and keeps us connected to the world. 

For more information: https://www.canada.ca/unitedstatestariffs</article>

items.title: Mind the Gap, please – AI Fairness in Health and the importance of diverse and inclusive data sets
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>This interview was taken in Vienna on February 11, 2025 on the International Day of Women and Girls in Science at the Austrian Academy of Science at an International Expert Panel Discussion organized by the diplomatic missions of Canada, France, Italy, Sweden, Switzerland, the Netherlands and the United Kingdom.</article>

items.title: How Graffiti+ Expanded to Overseas with Canada’s Trade Support a Conversation with Min. Ng
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>The honourable Mary Ng, Minister of Export Promotion, International Trade and Economic Development, sits down with Tim Matindas, Account Director at Graffiti+, a Canadian company successfully expanding into Japan, Korea, and Indonesia. In this conversation, Tim shares Graffiti+’s journey from a Canadian startup to an international player, highlighting the critical role of programs like the Trade Accelerator Program (TAP), CanExport, and Team Canada Trade Missions in breaking into new markets. He also provides practical insights for Canadian businesses looking to go global and explains how the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) helps companies build relationships, navigate foreign markets, and seize new opportunities.</article>

items.title: G7 2025 Short Logo Reveal
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>G7 2025 Kananaskis Short Logo Reveal
Showing scenic shots of a mountain in the Kananaskis area with a river and forest in the foreground, overlaid at the end with the G7 2025 Kananaskis logo featuring a grey stylized image of a mountain and a green pine tree with seven branches. The video is accompanied by background music.</article>

items.title: Team Canada Trade Missions – 2024 edition
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>In 2024, Canada led trade missions to Malaysia, Vietnam, South Korea, Indonesia, and the Philippines as part of Canada’s Indo-Pacific Strategy. 
 
These trade missions open doors for companies and organizations to broaden their reach and unlock new opportunities while helping Canada expand trade, investment, and supply chain resilience and deepening connections with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) and the broader Indo-Pacific region. 
 
Learn more about Canada’s Team Canada Trade Missions: https://www.international.gc.ca/trade-commerce/trade_events-evenements_commerciaux/trade_missions-missions_commerciales/about-a_propos.aspx?lang=eng  
 
#GoTeamCanadaTrade #IndoPacific #IndoPacificStrategy</article>

items.title: Minister Ng shares how CPTPP impacts Canadians and the world
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>In 2 weeks, Canada will welcome fellow Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) members in Vancouver for the #CPTPP Commission Meeting. 

As we look forward to this important event, listen in as Minister Ng shares how CPTPP impacts Canadians and the world.  

#CPTPPCanada2024</article>

items.title: Can we learn to spot disinformation?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Can we learn to spot disinformation? How can people protect themselves against disinformation?

We talked to Kathryn Hill, executive director of MediaSmarts, about digital literacy and how people can learn how to improve their disinformation detection skills. 

To learn more about Canada’s efforts to counter state-sponsored disinformation: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng

Watch Next: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLI-XuG0q34g1vTEhTCfYtl-80tPNtu5CA&si=rzSRMVpqF8oS95bS

00:55 How to avoid spreading disinformation
02:08 How disinformation works
02:59 The ecosystem of information</article>
